# Hypothesis Register

Use this as a live testing backlog. Each hypothesis should be tested with one major variable changed at a time where possible.

## A. Classification and search

| ID | Hypothesis | Primary metric |
|---:|---|---|
| H1 | Specific topic beats broad topic. | search traffic, saves |
| H2 | Keyword alignment across speech, caption, overlay, and title improves discovery. | search impressions, long-tail views |
| H3 | Narrow audience callout improves first retention. | 3-sec retention |
| H4 | Search-backed topics create longer tails than trend-backed topics. | 7d/30d views |
| H5 | One video should have one searchable promise. | average % viewed, search traffic |
| H6 | Platform-native terminology improves relevance. | search traffic, saves |

## B. First frame and scroll stop

| ID | Hypothesis | Primary metric |
|---:|---|---|
| H7 | Result-first frame beats context-first frame. | chose-to-view |
| H8 | Before/after split beats talking-head open for tutorial content. | early retention |
| H9 | Early face improves trust for small creators. | profile clicks, follows |
| H10 | Moderate face presence beats face-only content. | avg watch, saves |
| H11 | Motion in the first 0.5s reduces static-feed blindness. | swipe-away rate |
| H12 | Visible mistake/problem beats abstract claim. | early retention |
| H13 | Data/proof screenshot improves authority but may reduce mass appeal. | saves, follows |
| H14 | First-frame clutter increases skips. | 1-sec drop-off |

## C. Hooks

| ID | Hypothesis | Primary metric |
|---:|---|---|
| H15 | Problem-first hooks beat neutral educational hooks. | 3-sec hold |
| H16 | Contrarian hooks increase comments but can reduce trust if payoff is weak. | comments, completion |
| H17 | Proof-first hooks improve saves and follows. | saves, follows |
| H18 | Curiosity hooks improve completion only when payoff is visible. | completion |
| H19 | Identity hooks improve shares. | sends/shares |
| H20 | Cost-of-inaction hooks outperform benefit hooks for mistake-fixing content. | early retention |
| H21 | Question hooks underperform statement hooks unless highly specific. | chose-to-view |
| H22 | Hook-body mismatch boosts starts but hurts follow conversion. | follows per 1,000 views |

## D. Captions and text overlays

| ID | Hypothesis | Primary metric |
|---:|---|---|
| H23 | Edited captions beat auto captions. | avg % viewed |
| H24 | Short headline overlay beats no headline for ambiguous visuals. | 3-sec hold |
| H25 | Heavy central overlay hurts dynamic visuals. | completion |
| H26 | Labels beat full captions for visual comparison videos. | completion, saves |
| H27 | Navigation text increases completion in multi-step videos. | completion |
| H28 | Annotation arrows improve comprehension when visual target is small. | saves |
| H29 | Captions placed over the subject reduce retention. | completion |
| H30 | Keyword text in first 2s improves classification. | search traffic |
| H31 | Word-by-word captions increase stimulation but may reduce comprehension for complex ideas. | completion vs early retention |

## E. Visual rhythm and editing

| ID | Hypothesis | Primary metric |
|---:|---|---|
| H32 | Visual resets every 2–4s improve retention for educational Shorts. | avg % viewed |
| H33 | Every visual reset must add information. | completion, saves |
| H34 | Screen-recording zooms improve retention for software tutorials. | saves, avg watch |
| H35 | Side-by-side comparisons outperform sequential examples. | rewatch, saves |
| H36 | Proof inserts increase follow rate. | follows |
| H37 | Lo-fi/native style beats polished style in authenticity niches. | chose-to-view |
| H38 | Polished style beats lo-fi when credibility is the value proposition. | saves, follows |
| H39 | Rapid cuts hurt when viewers need to read or inspect. | saves, completion |

## F. Story and payoff

| ID | Hypothesis | Primary metric |
|---:|---|---|
| H40 | First payoff inside 3–7s improves retention. | avg watch |
| H41 | Delayed payoff improves completion only when tension is strong. | completion |
| H42 | Problem-test-result beats pure advice. | completion, saves |
| H43 | Before-after-why is strongest for visual edits. | saves, shares |
| H44 | Myth-evidence-correction drives comments and saves. | comments, saves |
| H45 | Teardown videos convert to follows better than generic tips. | follows |
| H46 | Open loops should be closed before the CTA. | completion, sentiment |
| H47 | More story beats per second beats more cuts per second. | avg watch |

## G. Save, send, comment, follow

| ID | Hypothesis | Primary metric |
|---:|---|---|
| H48 | Save CTAs work best on reference content. | saves |
| H49 | Send CTAs work best on identity/social content. | sends |
| H50 | Comment CTAs work best when the answer reveals segmentation. | comment quality |
| H51 | Follow CTAs are weak unless the series promise is clear. | follows |
| H52 | Relatable pain increases sends. | sends |
| H53 | Practical templates increase saves. | saves |
| H54 | Mild controversy increases comments but may reduce saves. | comments vs saves |
| H55 | Awe/surprise improves shares more safely than anger. | shares, sentiment |
| H56 | Specific-recipient CTAs beat generic share CTAs. | sends/shares |

## H. Platform-specific

| ID | Hypothesis | Platform | Primary metric |
|---:|---|---|---|
| H57 | Search-backed TikToks have longer tails than trend-backed TikToks. | TikTok | 7d/30d views |
| H58 | Full-watch optimisation matters more for short, single-payoff TikToks. | TikTok | full-watch rate |
| H59 | First frame acts like a thumbnail in Shorts. | YouTube Shorts | chose-to-view |
| H60 | Engaged views matter more than public views after the 2025 Shorts view-count change. | YouTube Shorts | engaged views |
| H61 | Searchable Shorts compound better than trend Shorts. | YouTube Shorts | 30d views |
| H62 | Trial Reels are best for testing new formats without follower bias. | Instagram | non-follower performance |
| H63 | Sends per reach is a key Reels growth signal. | Instagram | sends per reach |
| H64 | Follower content and non-follower content need different hooks. | Instagram | Trial Reel performance |

## I. Originality and trust

| ID | Hypothesis | Primary metric |
|---:|---|---|
| H65 | Original proof beats generic advice. | saves, follows |
| H66 | Creator judgment beats template volume. | follows, return viewers |
| H67 | Faceless content needs stronger proof than face-led content. | saves, follows |
| H68 | Reused clips require commentary that changes meaning. | trust, watch time |
| H69 | Series identity reduces slop perception. | follows, return viewers |
| H70 | Showing the experiment process builds more trust than only showing conclusions. | saves, profile clicks |
